title: Engagement Decision Protocol in Dimensional Human Field Theory (DHFT)
author: Tonucci, Cara
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The Engagement Decision Protocol (EDP) is a downstream operational protocol within the Structural Stability Science (SSS) / Dimensional Human Field Theory (DHFT) architecture. It applies Structural Identification Method (SIM) constraints to engagement decisions by evaluating whether external interactions preserve Load (L), Capacity (C), and Boundary (B) conditions. 
 EDP does not originate the mechanics it applies. The governing mechanics remain Load–Capacity–Boundary invariance as defined within SSS/DHFT/SIM. Downstream implementation, adaptation, software use, compliance use, governance use, or derivative summary of EDP must preserve canonical terminology, dependency order, and attribution to Cara Tonucci’s source records. 
 Canonical dependency order: Structural Stability Science (SSS) → Dimensional Human Field Theory (DHFT) → Structural Identification Method (SIM) → Load–Capacity–Boundary mechanics → Engagement Decision Protocol (EDP). 
 ___________ 
 This Protocol determines whether interaction with DHFT proceeds, redirects, or terminates. The protocol classifies interaction based on structural admissibility and assigns outcomes accordingly. Engagement occurs only under preservation of invariant variables, structural relations, and admissibility conditions. Non-admissible interaction routes to enforcement or non-engagement. The protocol eliminates discretionary response and preserves system integrity under interaction. 
 This document is part of the Dimensional Human Field Theory (DHFT) canonical system. 
 DHFT is a constraint-based system defined over invariant variables: Load (L), Capacity (C), and Boundary (B). 
 All valid system descriptions must preserve: - variable invariance   - structural relations   - admissibility conditions   
 This document defines a specific component within the DHFT system architecture.   It does not redefine system variables or introduce additional constructs. 
 Canonical system reference: Dimensional Human Field Theory (DHFT): Architectural Identification of a Closed Explanatory System   DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19075948   
 Validity and use constraints are defined in: Admissibility Conditions in DHFT   DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19572188
